destinations.evidence: FHE payload validation shares the 10 KiB input cap and base executor/TTL validation path.
Async detection records FHE because FHE is in the canonical async/SPC precompile metadata matrix.

equivalence_argument: The validation router applies the global 10 KiB cap before dispatching and routes FHE through validate_fhe_call_payload, which delegates to the shared executor request validation for nonzero executor and TTL bounds.
notes: This maps the bounded validation/detection invariant, not broader FHE wallet or delivery support.
rationale: The diff adds validate_fhe_call_payload with the same executor and TTL checks as other async payloads, and raises the global async input limit from 5 KiB to 10 KiB specifically because FHE DA-layer inputs are around 7 KiB. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: FHE async payload validation must decode the base executor fields, reject zero executor addresses, reject ttl == 0, reject ttl greater than max_ttl_blocks(), and async detection must allow inputs up to 10 KiB while rejecting larger inputs.
suggested_falsification_tests: Create FHE ABI inputs with zero executor, ttl 0, ttl max_ttl_blocks()+1, valid ttl/executor, a 7 KiB payload, and an over-10 KiB payload; assert validation/detection rejects only the invalid address, invalid TTLs, and over-limit input.
summary: FHE payload validation shares the async size, executor, and TTL bounds.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Create FHE ABI inputs with zero executor, ttl 0, ttl max_ttl_blocks()+1, valid ttl/executor, a 7 KiB payload, and an over-10 KiB payload; assert validation/detection rejects only the invalid address, invalid TTLs, and over-limit input.
title: FHE payloads share async validity bounds
